title: Offline Reinforcement Learning
summary: Learning from pre-collected data without interacting with the environment. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning / Learning Paradigms. Start after Reinforcement Learning Algorithms and Reinforcement Learning. It opens into AI in Offline Reinforcement Learning.

blocks.title: Compare and Contrast

panels.label: What it is
panels.body: Learning from pre-collected data without interacting with the environment.

panels.label: What it is not
panels.body: It is not just AI in Offline Reinforcement Learning and Reinforcement Learning Algorithms; the graph shows how it sits inside Reinforcement Learning.

panels.label: Common confusion
panels.body: Ask whether the term changes policy, reward, exploration, or control dynamics. Compare it against AI in Offline Reinforcement Learning and Reinforcement Learning Algorithms so the boundary stays visible instead of collapsing into a synonym pile.

panels.label: Best next step
panels.body: Review Reinforcement Learning Algorithms and Reinforcement Learning first, then move toward AI in Offline Reinforcement Learning.

blocks.takeaway: The comparison view keeps Offline Reinforcement Learning honest by showing the nearest neighbors, the real boundary, and the next study move.
